title: couroupita subsessilis
intro: Couroupita subsessilis, also known as the Couroupita or Kanthal, is a species of tree in the family Lecythidaceae, native to the tropical forests of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The medicinal properties of Couroupita subsessilis include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bark, leaves, and seeds. The health benefits of Couroupita subsessilis include its use in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Couroupita subsessilis include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often prepared by boiling its bark, leaves, or seeds in water and then applying the resulting liquid or solid to the affected area.
